subject: Ecology
title: Erosion
description: A quick, practical guide to Erosion.

hook.text: We lose 24 billion tons of topsoil every year—enough to fill 100 million freight trains.

buildup.text: Wind and water strip unprotected soil from farmland, hillsides, and construction sites.

discovery.text: It takes nature up to 1,000 years to build what erosion can remove in a single storm.

twist.text: The Dust Bowl of the 1930s stripped topsoil from millions of acres, displacing 2.5 million people.

climax.text: Cover crops, terracing, and no-till farming can slow erosion to nearly natural rates.

punchline.text: Soil blows away faster than it builds up.

quiz.question: How much topsoil is lost globally each year?
